questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A plane P makes intercepts with the axes, the sum of whose square is a constant equal to $\mathrm{K}^2$. The foot of the perpendicular from the origin $P$ is $(\lambda, \lambda, \lambda)$ the value of $\lambda$ in terms of $K$ is
questions.solution:
The normal to the plane will have direction cosine $\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}, \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}, \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)$ and the distance of this plane from the origin will be $\mathrm{OP}=\sqrt{3} \lambda$
$\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}+\frac{y}{\sqrt{3}}+\frac{z}{\sqrt{3}}=\sqrt{3} \lambda=x+y+z=3 \lambda$
Intercepts lengths $(3 \lambda, 3 \lambda, 3 \lambda)$
$\begin{aligned} & (3 \lambda)^2+(3 \lambda)^2+(3 \lambda)^2=\mathrm{k}^2 \\ & \lambda= \pm \frac{\mathrm{k}}{\sqrt{3}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The vector equation of a curve in $R^3$ denoted by $\vec{r}=\left(t^2+t-2\right) \hat{i}+(t+2) \hat{j}+(t+3)^2 \hat{k}$ denotes
options: a plane passing through the origin
a line parallel to the vector $\hat{i}+5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}$
a pair of lines intersecting at $(0,0,1)$
questions.solution:
Let $\vec{r}=x \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+z \hat{k}$
Comparing $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t}^2+\mathrm{t}-2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}=\mathrm{t}+2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{z}=\mathrm{t}^2+6 \mathrm{t}+9$
Hence $z-x=5 t+11 \Rightarrow z-x=5(y-2)+11 \Rightarrow x+5 y-z+1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
